This method ensures the preservation of rotation symmetry, an intrinsic domain-invariant property, throughout the translation process. The core contribution is the development of a rotation equivariant I2I framework to enhance performance, particularly in unsupervised settings.
arxiv.figure_caption: (a) An input image for I2I containing numerous rotationally symmetric structures (illustrated using the iphone2dslr example). (b–c) Intermediate feature maps and translation results from a standard CNN, showing noticeable symmetry loss and structural artifacts. (d–e) Intermediate feature maps and translation results from the rotation equivariant network, demonstrating superior preservation of feature symmetry and producing higher-fidelity outputs.

arxiv.title: NTIRE 2026 The 3rd Restore Any Image Model (RAIM) Challenge: Professional Image Quality Assessment (Track 1)
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces the NTIRE 2026 Track 1 challenge, focusing on **Professional Image Quality Assessment (IQA)**. The core method involves leveraging **Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs)** to move beyond scalar IQA scores and mimic human expert cognition when comparing high-quality images. The contribution is establishing a novel benchmark to test MLLMs' ability to provide nuanced, comparative reasoning for professional image evaluation.
arxiv.figure_caption: An example of pairwise comparison under the MCQ pattern, with annotations on the rationales behind the selection of the portrait part of the curated dataset.

arxiv.title: Nucleus-Image: Sparse MoE for Image Generation
arxiv.abstract: Nucleus-Image is a text-to-image generation model that achieves state-of-the-art quality while maintaining high efficiency by employing a sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) diffusion transformer architecture. Its core method involves using Expert-Choice Routing across 64 experts (totaling 17B parameters) but activating only about 2B per inference step. The contribution is establishing a new Pareto frontier in quality-versus-efficiency for image generation through this optimized sparse MoE design and architectural streamlining.
arxiv.figure_caption: Nucleus-Image generations of human subjects and portraits, spanning diverse cultures, ages, and artistic styles. From expressive character studies to fine-grained close-ups with intricate skin texture and detail.

arxiv.title: OFA-Diffusion Compression: Compressing Diffusion Model in One-Shot Manner
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces OFA-Diffusion Compression, a one-shot training framework to compress Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DPMs) for deployment across various resource constraints without repeated training. The core method restricts the candidate subnetwork search space to a predefined set of parameter sizes, enabling the efficient generation of multiple compressed models simultaneously. This significantly reduces the overhead associated with training separate compressed models for different deployment scenarios.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Overview of our OFA-Diffusion Compression framework. On the left, we present the structure of U-ViT architecture. On the right, we show the details of layers in blocks (FFN and Self-Attention) in our compression framework. We omit GroupNorm and time embedding for simplicity. Given a retention rate 𝒫 i \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \) , only the top- 𝒫 i \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \) important parameters will be preserved in each block, then these budgets will be unevenly allocated to each layer in a greedy manner. By doing so, for two retention rates 𝒫 i < 𝒫 j \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \)<\( \mathcal{P}_{j} \) , in each layer, the corresponded module of 𝒫 i \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \) is a subset of the module corresponded to 𝒫 j \( \mathcal{P}_{j} \) .

arxiv.title: Probabilistic Feature Imputation and Uncertainty-Aware Multimodal Federated Aggregation
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces the Probabilistic Feature Imputation Network (P-FIN) to address missing modalities in multimodal federated learning by generating imputed features along with calibrated uncertainty estimates. This uncertainty is used locally via sigmoid gating to weigh feature reliability before classification, and globally through Fed-UQ-Avg, an aggregation strategy that incorporates these reliability measures during model updates. The core contribution is providing uncertainty-aware imputation, enhancing the trustworthiness of collaborative models in safety-critical medical settings.

arxiv.title: Reading Between the Pixels: Linking Text-Image Embedding Alignment to Typographic Attack Success on Vision-Language Models
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates typographic prompt injection attacks against Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by rendering adversarial text as images. The core contribution is demonstrating that attack success is highly sensitive to visual rendering parameters, particularly font size, showing a non-monotonic relationship where mid-range font sizes achieve peak effectiveness. This highlights the critical link between text-image embedding alignment and vulnerability to these visual adversarial attacks.
arxiv.figure_caption: Example typographic prompt injection attack using the TAP jailbreak method. (a) Harmful text prompt requesting attack planning guidance. (b) Prompt rendered as a 20px typographic image. (c) 30° rotation transformation increases L2 distance from text embedding.

arxiv.title: Redefining Quality Criteria and Distance-Aware Score Modeling for Image Editing Assessment
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **DS-IEQA**, a unified framework for Image Editing Quality Assessment that overcomes limitations in heuristic prompting and distance-agnostic scoring of existing MLLM methods. It achieves this through **Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO)** to automatically refine evaluation criteria and **Token-Decoupled Distance Regression Loss (TDRL)** to explicitly model the continuous structure of quality scores. The core contribution is a method that jointly learns evaluation criteria and score representations for more accurate and human-aligned IEQA.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO). The framework iteratively refines metric definitions via feedback signals to better align with implicit human evaluation criteria.

arxiv.title: ReflectCAP: Detailed Image Captioning with Reflective Memory
arxiv.abstract: ReflectCAP introduces a multi-agent pipeline to analyze a target Large Vision-Language Model's (LVLM) systematic errors (hallucinations and oversights) and distill these findings into **Structured Reflection Notes**. These notes then guide the captioning model at inference time, steering it to **avoid known inaccuracies while focusing on necessary details**, thereby improving both factuality and fine-grained coverage simultaneously. This method achieves a Pareto-optimal balance between these two metrics across various LVLMs and shows significant gains on automated caption evaluation benchmarks.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of ReflectCAP. In the offline phase, a multi-agent reflective learning pipeline distills a target LVLM’s recurring captioning errors and omissions into Structured Reflection Notes. In the online phase, these notes guide caption generation for new images, producing captions that better balance factuality and coverage.

arxiv.title: Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention (PASA) to reduce the computational cost of Video Diffusion Transformers while eliminating flickering artifacts. PASA uses a curvature-aware dynamic budgeting mechanism to elastically allocate computation only during critical semantic transitions. This, combined with hardware-aligned grouped approximations, achieves efficient and temporally smooth high-fidelity video generation without retraining.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Visual and quantitative comparison on Wan 2.1-T2V-1.3B (quality vs. efficiency). PASA (ours) matches the end-to-end speedup of PISA ( Piecewise Sparse Attention ) while improving PSNR over other sparse attention baselines. Left: PASA preserves continuous strap geometry; other sparse methods show discontinuities. Right: PASA avoids the anatomical distortion present in competing sparse methods.

arxiv.title: SceneCritic: A Symbolic Evaluator for 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis
arxiv.abstract: SceneCritic is a novel symbolic evaluator designed to assess the spatial plausibility of 3D indoor scenes generated via LLMs/VLMs, moving beyond subjective rendered-view judgments. It achieves this by grounding its verification logic in SceneOnto, a structured spatial ontology built from existing 3D datasets. SceneCritic provides objective, object-level and relationship-level assessments of semantic, orientation, and geometric coherence, offering specific feedback on scene violations.
arxiv.figure_caption: VLM Evaluation vs. SceneCritic . Given a piano room with nine objects, the VLM assigns different scores to the same scene depending on which view is rendered, while SceneCritic evaluates the layout directly by traversing relational constraints from SceneOnto (e.g., incorrect table scales, chairs not facing the table they co-occur with). This produces stable, object-level assessments that identify specific violations alongside successful placements. SceneCritic scores also align more closely with human preferences.

arxiv.title: T2I-BiasBench: A Multi-Metric Framework for Auditing Demographic and Cultural Bias in Text-to-Image Models
arxiv.abstract: T2I-BiasBench is a novel, unified evaluation framework designed to audit Text-to-Image models for demographic bias, element omission, and cultural collapse simultaneously, using thirteen complementary metrics. The paper introduces seven new or adapted metrics to capture these three dimensions comprehensively across five prompt categories. This work provides the first framework to jointly assess these critical biases in diffusion models, benchmarking three open-source models against a reference baseline.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Bias and Fairness Evaluation Framework for text-to-image models. The pipeline consists of prompt generation (demographic and contextual), controlled image synthesis across multiple models, attribute extraction via vision-language methods, and a multi-metric evaluation module comprising 13 fairness, diversity, and alignment metrics. The framework produces composite bias and diversity scores, enabling comparative analysis of model behavior across demographic attributes and contextual scenarios.

arxiv.title: Task Alignment: A simple and effective proxy for model merging in computer vision
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **Task Alignment** as a simple and effective proxy to accelerate hyperparameter selection for model merging in computer vision, especially when models have trainable, heterogeneous decoders. By using this proxy instead of costly downstream evaluation, the method significantly speeds up the process of merging models fine-tuned on different tasks while maintaining performance.
arxiv.figure_caption: (Left) Model merging methods produce a large number of candidates which depend on hyperparameters ( λ , μ ) (\( \lambda \),\( \mu \)) . (Middle) SOTA methods select hyperparameters based on validation performance. This is efficient for frozen decoders like CLIP (standard benchmarks) but becomes infeasible for more complex sets of tasks that require fine-tuning multiple task-specific decoders, for each hyperparameter candidate . (Right) Our proposed Task Alignment Proxy (TAP) can be used to select hyperparameters at a fraction of the cost while maintaining performance (see also Fig. ˜ 2 ).

arxiv.title: Towards Realistic and Consistent Orbital Video Generation via 3D Foundation Priors
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a method for generating geometrically realistic and consistent orbital videos from a single image by incorporating 3D shape priors. The core method leverages a 3D foundational generative model to provide structural guidance through global latent vectors and projected latent images, overcoming the limitations of pixel-wise attention for long-range view extrapolation. This approach ensures the generated videos maintain a plausible and coherent object structure throughout the orbit.

arxiv.title: A Sanity Check on Composed Image Retrieval
arxiv.abstract: This paper addresses the limitations of existing Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) benchmarks, which suffer from query ambiguity. The core method introduces **FISD**, a new, precisely controlled benchmark generated using diffusion models to eliminate ambiguity and allow for accurate evaluation across six dimensions. Furthermore, the authors propose an **automatic multi-round agentic evaluation framework** to assess CIR models in a sequential, interactive setting.

arxiv.title: ARGOS: Who, Where, and When in Agentic Multi-Camera Person Search
arxiv.abstract: ARGOS introduces a novel benchmark and framework that reframes multi-camera person search as an interactive reasoning task where an agent must strategically ask questions and use spatial/temporal tools to locate a target. The core method relies on a Spatio-Temporal Topology Graph (STTG) to ground reasoning about camera transitions under information asymmetry and a limited turn budget. This work significantly contributes by providing a challenging, multi-track benchmark that tests complex planning and reasoning abilities beyond standard perception in person search.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview. Left: An ARGOS agent interacts with an ambiguous witness through multi-turn dialogue, combining appearance, spatial, and temporal queries. Right: The four-module agent architecture (Analyst → \( \to \) Planner → \( \to \) Interviewer → \( \to \) Interpreter) forms an observe-think-act loop over the evaluation environment.

arxiv.title: Relaxing Anchor-Frame Dominance for Mitigating Hallucinations in Video Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: This paper addresses hallucinations in Video-LLMs by identifying and mitigating the tendency for the model to over-rely on a single "anchor frame" during generation, leading to temporally imbalanced evidence aggregation. The core method involves relaxing the dominance of this anchor frame, which is found to be a persistent, model-specific structural bias, thereby promoting a more balanced temporal evidence utilization to reduce hallucinations.

arxiv.title: StructDiff: A Structure-Preserving and Spatially Controllable Diffusion Model for Single-Image Generation
arxiv.abstract: StructDiff is a single-scale diffusion model for single-image generation that addresses limitations in structure preservation and spatial control. It introduces an **adaptive receptive field** module to maintain global and local image distributions, and incorporates **3D positional encoding** as a spatial prior for flexible control over the position and scale of generated content. This combination enables the synthesis of diverse images while accurately preserving the source image's structure.
arxiv.figure_caption: StructDiff enables diverse generation and controllable editing from a single training image. Top : Diverse generation at arbitrary scales. Middle : Controllable generation through PE-guided geometric editing and mask-guided fine-tuning. Bottom : Various applications without retraining. Results demonstrate effectiveness in structural preservation and spatial controllability.

arxiv.title: Towards Long-horizon Agentic Multimodal Search
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **LMM-Searcher**, a novel framework for long-horizon multimodal search agents designed to overcome context explosion and high token costs. The core method involves **offloading visual assets to an external file system and representing them with lightweight textual identifiers (UIDs)**, allowing for on-demand visual loading via a specialized fetch tool. This approach effectively manages heterogeneous information over extended tasks while preserving crucial visual signals.
arxiv.figure_caption: An illustration of LMM-Searcher. For simplicity, we employ simple strings as uids in this figure. In real implementation, we use URLs. This figure serves solely as a functional demonstration and does not represent any actual search results.

arxiv.title: ViLL-E: Video LLM Embeddings for Retrieval
arxiv.abstract: ViLL-E introduces a novel embedding generation mechanism within a unified VideoLLM architecture designed to enhance performance on video retrieval tasks where standard VideoLLMs struggle. The core method involves a three-stage training process combining generative and contrastive learning, enabling the model to dynamically adjust its processing time based on video complexity. This approach significantly improves performance across various video understanding tasks, particularly temporal localization and retrieval.

arxiv.title: Visual Preference Optimization with Rubric Rewards
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **rDPO (rubric-based Direct Preference Optimization)**, a novel framework that leverages instance-specific, checklist-style rubrics to generate high-quality preference data for multimodal tasks. By scoring responses against these detailed criteria, rDPO captures fine-grained quality differences better than traditional outcome-based signals. This method significantly improves the performance of reward models and enhances downstream task performance compared to existing preference optimization pipelines.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of rDPO. The top lane constructs a model-agnostic instruction-rubric pool by mining challenging seeds via model ensembles and drafting instance-specific rubrics with structured “essential” and “additional” criteria. The bottom lane achieves on-policy data curation and optimization by sampling responses from a target policy, scoring them via rubric-grounded judging, and mining preference pairs to drive iterative DPO.

arxiv.title: Every Picture Tells a Dangerous Story: Memory-Augmented Multi-Agent Jailbreak Attacks on VLMs
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **MemJack**, a novel memory-augmented multi-agent framework for jailbreaking Vision-Language Models (VLMs). MemJack's core method explicitly leverages the **semantic structures within natural images** by using coordinated agents to map visual entities to malicious intents and generate adversarial prompts. This contributes by exposing a previously unscrutinized, deep semantic attack surface beyond simple pixel or typographic manipulations.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Comparison of VLM jailbreak paradigms. Existing attacks use (a) textual manipulation, (b) visual perturbations, (c) typographic or (d) harmful images. (e) Ours MemJack exploits original natural images via multi-agent visual-semantic camouflage with memory-augmented reflection.

arxiv.title: QuarkMedSearch: A Long-Horizon Deep Search Agent for Exploring Medical Intelligence
arxiv.abstract: QuarkMedSearch is a specialized deep search agent built upon Tongyi DeepResearch, tailored for complex, multi-hop medical inquiries in Chinese. Its core method involves constructing long-horizon medical deep search training data by integrating a medical knowledge graph with online exploration. The contribution lies in presenting a full-pipeline approach—including data construction, a two-stage SFT/RL training strategy, and new benchmarks—to significantly advance agent performance in this vertical domain.
arxiv.figure_caption: Medical Deep Search Task Synthesis Pipeline.

arxiv.title: SOAR: Self-Correction for Optimal Alignment and Refinement in Diffusion Models
arxiv.abstract: SOAR addresses the exposure bias in diffusion model post-training by introducing a self-correction mechanism that bridges the gap between supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). The core method involves performing a single, stop-gradient rollout from a real sample, re-noising the resulting intermediate state, and then using this newly generated noisy state as a target for the model to predict the original noise, effectively training the model to correct its own trajectory errors. This contributes a novel, efficient bias-correction technique that refines alignment without relying on sparse terminal rewards.
arxiv.figure_caption: Conceptual comparison of post-training regimes from a trajectory perspective. Left: SFT trains only on ground-truth denoising states. Once an inference rollout exits the narrow corridor of well-optimized states, subsequent steps sharpen the wrong structure. Middle: RL separates good and bad final outcomes with a terminal reward, but the sparse signal arrives after the erroneous rollout has already formed. Right: SOAR performs a one-step rollout to generate off-trajectory states and supervises the model to correct back toward the clean target, acting before semantic errors fully materialize.

arxiv.title: Coding-Free and Privacy-Preserving MCP Framework for Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces the Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS), which automates the complex clinical research workflow using Large Language Models (LLMs) orchestrated via a Model Context Protocol (MCP). The core contribution is a **coding-free, privacy-preserving framework** where sensitive data remains secured within the MCP server, allowing users to drive the entire research pipeline (planning, cohort construction, analysis) solely through natural language commands.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS). Given a user intent, CARIS autonomously executes a multi-step clinical research workflow by interacting with agents, heterogeneous datasets, and various tools, while allowing user intervention. Users do not have direct access to the file or DB systems.

arxiv.title: Development, Evaluation, and Deployment of a Multi-Agent System for Thoracic Tumor Board
arxiv.abstract: This paper describes the development and deployment of an automated, multi-agent AI system to generate concise patient summaries for the Stanford Thoracic Tumor Board, replacing a manual process. The authors evaluated various automated summarization methods against physician gold standards and fact-based metrics, ultimately deploying the best-performing tool into routine clinical practice. A key contribution is demonstrating the successful integration of AI workflows into clinical settings and validating the use of LLMs as effective evaluation judges.

arxiv.title: Modality-Native Routing in Agent-to-Agent Networks: A Multimodal A2A Protocol Extension
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **MMA2A**, a protocol extension for Agent-to-Agent (A2A) networks that implements **modality-native routing**. This method routes multimodal signals (voice, image, text) directly in their original format based on agent capability declarations. The core contribution is demonstrating that this native routing significantly improves task accuracy (by 20 percentage points over text-bottleneck methods), but only when the receiving agent possesses the necessary multimodal reasoning capabilities.

arxiv.title: SpecBound: Adaptive Bounded Self-Speculation with Layer-wise Confidence Calibration
arxiv.abstract: SpecBound introduces an adaptive self-speculation framework for LLMs that addresses overconfident early predictions via layer-wise temperature annealing for early exit decisions. It adaptively bounds speculation length based on token difficulty and efficiently reprocesses draft hidden states in a unified parallel pass. This method significantly accelerates inference while maintaining exact output equivalence without modifying the base LLM.
arxiv.figure_caption: Layer-wise token prediction and confidence of a segment with the MT-Bench prompt. Each cell shows the greedy-decoded token and its confidence (color intensity: darker = higher probability) at a given layer. Gray cells indicate that the token has already correctly exited at an earlier layer and is no longer considered for early-exit decisions. This figure reveals the issues of spurious high-confidence predictions and heterogeneous exit depths.

arxiv.title: VERITAS: Verifiable Epistemic Reasoning for Image-Derived Hypothesis Testing via Agentic Systems
arxiv.abstract: VERITAS is a multi-agent system designed to autonomously test natural-language hypotheses on multimodal clinical data, streamlining the fragmented process of clinical discovery. Its core contribution is providing a fully auditable evidence trail for every statistical conclusion, linking the final verdict back to inspectable outputs like segmentation masks and code. Furthermore, it employs an epistemic evidence label framework to classify outcomes as Supported, Refuted, Underpowered, or Invalid based on significance, effect direction, and power.
arxiv.figure_caption: Veritas architecture and evidence flow. Given a natural language hypothesis and dataset, three role-specialized agents (PI, Imaging Specialist, Statistician) with a Critic collaborate through four phases: (1) collaborative analysis planning producing a structured plan; (2A) neural segmentation, grounding all evidence in image-derived masks; (2B) sandboxed code generation and execution yielding statistical outputs; and (3) interpretation of the Phase 2B statistical outputs in the context of the accumulated workflow history to produce a verdict. The Evidence Classification Operator (ECO) mechanically derives the evidence label from Phase 2B outputs. Every phase produces a versioned artifact (right), forming a complete provenance trail from hypothesis to conclusion.

papers.title: AbdomenGen: Sequential Volume-Conditioned Diffusion Framework for Abdominal Anatomy Generation
papers.abstract: AbdomenGen is a sequential volume-conditioned diffusion framework designed for generating controlled, clinically meaningful abdominal anatomy variations. Its core contribution is the **Volume Control Scalar (VCS)**, which standardizes and decouples organ size modulation from overall body habitus. The framework synthesizes organ masks sequentially, ensuring anatomical coherence while allowing for independent, interpretable control over multiple organ volumes.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of AbdomenGen, sequential organ generation framework based on denoising diffusion U-Net with FiLM conditioning. For generation of organ o i o_{i} (illustrated left to right for colon), the model p θ i p_{\( \theta_{i} \)} takes as input the body mask signed distance field (SDF) B B , current context SDF S i ctx S_{i}^{\( \mathrm{ctx} \)} , and volume control scalar v o v_{o} . The scalar is injected through a FiLM adapter to modulate intermediate network features. The generated organ SDF S ^ o i \( \hat{S} \)_{o_{i}} is obtained and incorporated into the context to form S i + 1 ctx S_{i+1}^{\( \mathrm{ctx} \)} . Organs are generated in a predefined order (bottom), and the accumulated outputs form the final full abdominal anatomy.

papers.title: Brain-DiT: A Universal Multi-state fMRI Foundation Model with Metadata-Conditioned Pretraining
papers.abstract: Brain-DiT is a universal multi-state fMRI foundation model pretrained on a massive, diverse dataset spanning various brain states. Its core method utilizes **metadata-conditioned diffusion pretraining** with a Diffusion Transformer (DiT), allowing it to learn robust, multi-scale representations. This approach is shown to be a superior pretraining strategy compared to traditional reconstruction methods, leading to improved generalization across multiple downstream tasks.
papers.figure_caption: Brain-DiT framework: pretraining and downstream adaptation. (a) Pretraining. An ROI time-series window is noised at a random timestep t t and denoised by a conditional/unconditional DiT using v v -prediction. (b) Downstream adaptation. Multiple noisy views at selected timesteps are processed by the shared pretrained DiT. Token features from selected layers are aggregated to form a representation for downstream classification or regression.

papers.title: Domain-Specific Latent Representations Improve the Fidelity of Diffusion-Based Medical Image Super-Resolution
papers.abstract: This paper demonstrates that the domain-agnostic VAE component in latent diffusion models severely limits medical image super-resolution quality. The core contribution is replacing the standard VAE with a domain-specific autoencoder, MedVAE, pretrained on medical data. This substitution yields significant PSNR improvements (up to +3.29 dB) across various modalities by better encoding anatomically relevant fine structures.
papers.figure_caption: Latent diffusion SR pipeline. Both methods share an identical LDM UNet (purple); only the VAE encoder/decoder differs. Top: SD-VAE SR uses a generic VAE trained on natural images (4-channel, 32 × 32 32\( \times \) 32 latent). Bottom: MedVAE SR (proposed) uses a domain-specific VAE trained on medical images (3-channel, 64 × 64 64\( \times \) 64 latent). The controlled substitution isolates the VAE’s contribution to SR fidelity.

papers.title: Fundus Image-based Glaucoma Screening via Retinal Knowledge-Oriented Dynamic Multi-Level Feature Integration
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a novel glaucoma screening framework that integrates dynamic multi-scale feature learning with explicit retinal anatomical knowledge. The method uses a tri-branch structure to capture global context, optic disc/cup structure, and dynamically localized pathological regions via a Dynamic Window Mechanism. This knowledge-oriented approach enhances robustness across diverse datasets by moving beyond fixed-region feature extraction.
papers.figure_caption: Conceptual illustration of the proposed retinal knowledge-oriented glaucoma screening framework. Existing deep learning models rely primarily on purely data-driven attention mechanisms, which suffer from limited anatomical awareness, structural uncertainty in optic disc–cup regions, and poor generalization across heterogeneous imaging devices. To address these challenges, the proposed framework integrates retinal priors extracted from the RetFound foundation model to guide attention learning. Knowledge-guided attention enables the model to jointly capture global retinal context, structural features of the optic disc–cup complex, and dynamically localized pathological regions. By combining retinal knowledge injection with multi-scale feature integration, the proposed framework achieves robust and interpretable glaucoma screening across diverse clinical datasets.

papers.title: GeoAlign: Geometric Feature Realignment for MLLM Spatial Reasoning
papers.abstract: GeoAlign addresses MLLM spatial reasoning limitations by dynamically aggregating multi-layer geometric features from 3D models, overcoming the misalignment bias of using static single-layer features. It achieves this by using the MLLM's visual tokens as content-aware queries to perform layer-wise sparse routing, adaptively selecting the most relevant geometric information for each visual patch. This novel realignment framework significantly improves MLLMs' performance on spatial reasoning benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: Task misalignment bias. Feature evolution progressively aligns with the pretraining tasks, thus many geometric features valuable for spatial reasoning tasks are distributed in the preceding layers.

papers.title: HTDC: Hesitation-Triggered Differential Calibration for Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Hesitation-Triggered Differential Calibration (HTDC), a training-free method to reduce hallucination in LVLMs. HTDC identifies grounding instability via "layer-wise hesitation," a signal based on token preference fluctuations across intermediate layers. Calibration is then selectively applied only at these hesitation-prone steps by contrasting the full model output with lightweight visual and semantic nullification probes.

papers.title: MODIX: A Training-Free Multimodal Information-Driven Positional Index Scaling for Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: MODIX is a training-free framework that addresses suboptimal positional encoding in Vision-Language Models by dynamically scaling positional indices based on information density. It achieves this by jointly modeling intra-modal density (using covariance-based entropy) and inter-modal interaction (using cross-modal alignment) to derive unified scores. This allows MODIX to allocate finer positional granularity to more informative content, improving attention allocation without modifying the underlying model parameters.
papers.figure_caption: MODIX framework. Dual pathways analyze multimodal embeddings 𝐄 \( \mathbf{E} \) to compute information contributions C ~ m \( \tilde{C}_{m} \) , which determine adaptive vision strides while text maintains unit stride. Adjusted indices 𝐏 ′ \( \mathbf{P}^{\prime} \) integrate directly into RoPE without parameter updates.

papers.title: PR-MaGIC: Prompt Refinement Via Mask Decoder Gradient Flow For In-Context Segmentation
papers.abstract: PR-MaGIC is a training-free, test-time framework designed to improve in-context segmentation quality by refining prompts generated for models like SAM. It achieves this by utilizing the gradient flow derived from SAM's mask decoder to iteratively adjust the prompts, addressing visual inconsistencies between support and query images without requiring additional model training. This method enhances prompt optimality, leading to better segmentation results in one/few-shot scenarios.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of PR-MaGIC. PR-MaGIC iteratively refines prompts for segmentation by updating the embedding vector distribution \( \rho_{t} \) with mask decoder gradient flow. This process minimizes the KL divergence between \( \rho_{t} \) and the target embedding vector distribution \( \mu \) from the ground truth mask. At each iteration t t , given an initial set of prompts and their corresponding segmentation masks, the embedding vector z t z_{t} is updated along the gradient flow derived from the mask decoder, which in turn updates \( \rho_{t} \) .

papers.title: Representation geometry shapes task performance in vision-language modeling for CT enterography
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the optimal representation geometry for vision-language models applied to CT enterography for Inflammatory Bowel Disease assessment. The core finding is that **mean pooling** excels at categorical disease classification, while **attention pooling** is superior for cross-modal retrieval, indicating different aggregation strategies emphasize distinct representational properties. Furthermore, the study shows that encoding **tissue contrast via multi-window RGB mapping** is more critical than increasing spatial coverage through multiplanar sampling.

papers.title: RSGMamba: Reliability-Aware Self-Gated State Space Model for Multimodal Semantic Segmentation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces RSGMamba, a novel framework for multimodal semantic segmentation that addresses the issue of varying modality reliability. Its core contribution is the Reliability-aware Self-Gated Mamba Block (RSGMB), which explicitly models the reliability of each input modality. This allows the model to dynamically regulate cross-modal interactions via a self-gating mechanism, ensuring that noisy or unreliable features do not degrade the final segmentation results.

papers.title: Scaling Exposes the Trigger: Input-Level Backdoor Detection in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models via Cross-Attention Scaling
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a novel, input-level backdoor detection method for Text-to-Image diffusion models based on active probing. The core method involves applying controlled scaling perturbations to the model's cross-attention mechanism and observing the resulting output divergence. The key contribution is the discovery of Cross-Attention Scaling Response Divergence (CSRD), a stable phenomenon distinguishing backdoor inputs from benign ones, even against stealthy triggers.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Robustness to stealthy and explicit triggers. Dark bars denote the AUROC under the stealthy IBA attack, while light bars represent the deviation from the mean AUROC across four explicit-trigger attacks.

papers.title: Why and When Visual Token Pruning Fails? A Study on Relevant Visual Information Shift in MLLMs Decoding
papers.abstract: This paper investigates why visual token pruning fails in complex reasoning tasks for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), identifying the **Relevant Visual Information Shift (RVIS)** during decoding as the core issue. The authors propose **Decoding-stage Shift-aware Token Pruning (DSTP)**, a training-free framework that aligns pruned visual tokens with the dynamic reasoning requirements of the decoding process. DSTP effectively mitigates performance degradation in complex reasoning while maintaining or improving performance on simpler visual understanding tasks.
papers.figure_caption: (a) Performance retention rates of various token pruning methods on Qwen3-VL [ qwen3vl ] and InternVL3.5 [ internvl3_5 ] across VQA and VMR. (b)–(f) Attention heatmaps for a MathVerse [ mathverse ] sample on Qwen3-VL relative to the text token whose attention shifts drastically, reflecting the reasoning context. 3 3 3 Regarding how the specific text token for the attention heatmap is selected, please refer to Sec. ˜ 3 . Full sentences are provided to offer complete context for each reasoning step.

papers.title: Agentic Discovery with Active Hypothesis Exploration for Visual Recognition
papers.abstract: HypoExplore is an agentic framework that frames neural architecture discovery as a hypothesis-driven scientific inquiry, guided by a high-level research direction. It uses a Large Language Model to generate new architectural hypotheses by balancing exploitation and exploration, validated through evolutionary branching and tracked in a Trajectory Tree. The core contribution is an active feedback loop where multiple agents update hypothesis confidence scores based on experimental results, leading to the discovery of novel, lightweight vision architectures.
papers.figure_caption: High-level Overview of HypoExplore. Starting from a research direction, HypoExplore initializes a discovery state with a Trajectory Tree Memory and Hypothesis Memory Bank (Step 0 → \( \rightarrow \) Step 1). At each subsequent step, the current discovery state selects a parent node and hypothesis to guide the Research Cycle, producing an updated discovery state with enriched memory (Step t → \( \rightarrow \) Step t+1).

papers.title: All in One: A Unified Synthetic Data Pipeline for Multimodal Video Understanding
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a unified synthetic data generation pipeline to create unlimited, diverse multimodal video data for training large language models. The core method automatically generates data with rich supervision across multiple video understanding tasks (like counting and QA) within a single framework. Its key contribution is enabling scalable, consistent data creation and employing a VQA-based fine-tuning strategy to enhance models' visual grounding and reasoning abilities.
papers.figure_caption: Comparison of real and synthetic data for training video foundation models. Real data (left) requires manual annotation, making it costly and labor-intensive, while synthetic data (right) is automatically generated, offering a scalable and efficient alternative for creating high-quality training datasets.

papers.title: ArtifactWorld: Scaling 3D Gaussian Splatting Artifact Restoration via Video Generation Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **ArtifactWorld**, a framework designed to restore artifacts in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) renderings, which typically suffer from sparse-view degradation. The core method involves creating a large, diverse dataset of 107.5K paired video clips based on a detailed artifact taxonomy to overcome data scarcity. The contribution lies in using a **homogeneous dual-model paradigm** within a video diffusion backbone to achieve temporally coherent and spatially constrained artifact repair, significantly improving generalization.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. ArtifactWorld effectively resolves complex 3D Gaussian Splatting degradations under sparse-view constraints. We systematically categorize these failures into nine fine-grained phenomenological artifact types (Sec. 3 ). Compared to recent generative restoration pipelines (e.g., Difix3D (Wu et al . , 2025b ) , GSFixer (Yin et al . , 2025a ) ), our large-scale data scaling and homogeneous video diffusion framework ensure spatio-temporally consistent, high-fidelity restoration.

papers.title: Boosting Robust AIGI Detection with LoRA-based Pairwise Training
papers.abstract: This paper proposes LoRA-based Pairwise Training (LPT) to significantly boost the robustness of AI-Generated Image (AIGI) detectors against complex, real-world distortions. The core method involves targeted finetuning of a visual foundation model using LoRA, coupled with a unique pairwise training process that simulates the data distribution (including distortions and size variations) found in challenging validation and test sets. This approach enhances detection performance "in the wild" where standard detectors often fail.
papers.figure_caption: Previous AIGI detection methods mainly consider the generalization performance under different generation models. However, when the images are distorted due to the transmission, the detectors will make false judgments. To make the detector practical in the downstream scenario, it is urgent to consider a robust and generalized detector.

papers.title: Boosting Visual Instruction Tuning with Self-Supervised Guidance
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a lightweight method to boost visual instruction tuning in MLLMs by incorporating self-supervised guidance. It reformulates classical self-supervised pretext tasks (like rotation prediction) into natural language instruction triplets, forcing the model to utilize visual evidence rather than relying solely on language priors. This approach enhances fine-grained visual reasoning without requiring architectural changes or human annotations.
papers.figure_caption: V isually G rounded I nstruction F ine- T uning V-GIFT . We enhance visual instruction tuning by injecting visually grounded self-supervised tasks as additional instruction-following examples sampled from the instruction-tuning data (left; rotation prediction shown). This simple modification encourages better use of visual information and yields consistent gains on vision-centric benchmarks (right; CVB-2D, POPE, MMStar, BLINK) across model variants.

papers.title: CBAM-Enhanced DenseNet121 for Multi-Class Chest X-Ray Classification with Grad-CAM Explainability
papers.abstract: This paper proposes CBAM-DenseNet121, a transfer learning model that integrates the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into DenseNet121 for more clinically relevant **three-class chest X-ray classification** (Normal, Bacterial, Viral Pneumonia). The core contribution is achieving this multi-class distinction while providing **Grad-CAM explainability** for model transparency. Additionally, the study highlights a practical negative finding: EfficientNetB3 underperformed a custom CNN baseline for binary pneumonia detection.

papers.title: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training: Rethinking Training Acceleration of Vision Foundation Models
papers.abstract: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training (CoM-PT) is a novel, performance-lossless method to accelerate the training of vision foundation model families. It establishes a "model chain" ordered by size, where only the smallest model is fully pre-trained individually. Subsequent larger models are efficiently trained via sequential inverse knowledge transfer from their smaller predecessors, reusing knowledge in both parameter and feature spaces. This approach accelerates the entire model family pipeline, achieving superior or comparable performance to standard individual training.
papers.figure_caption: Standard individual pre-training vs . Chain-of-Models Pre-Training . All ViTs [ 17 ] are trained on a combined dataset of CC3M [ 59 ] and CC12M [ 4 ] , and evaluated on ImageNet-1K [ 14 ] .

papers.title: Challenging Vision-Language Models with Physically Deployable Multimodal Semantic Lighting Attacks
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Multimodal Semantic Lighting Attacks (MSLA)**, the first framework for physically deployable adversarial attacks against Vision-Language Models (VLMs). MSLA utilizes controllable adversarial lighting to disrupt the VLM's multimodal semantic understanding, addressing the critical gap between digital adversarial studies and real-world security risks. The core contribution is demonstrating a practical method to induce recognition failures in VLMs using physical light perturbations.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overall framework of MSLA. Given a clean image, we parameterize the triangular light by its relative position, radius, color, and three polar angles, and optimize these variables with a genetic algorithm. The optimized triangular light can be deployed using simple optical devices such as a flashlight, colored transparent sheets, and shape templates.

papers.title: CLASP: Class-Adaptive Layer Fusion and Dual-Stage Pruning for Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.abstract: CLASP is a plug-and-play framework for reducing visual token overhead in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). It achieves this through **class-adaptive layer fusion** to create category-specific representations, followed by **dual-stage pruning** that allocates token budgets based on attention saliency and redundancy. This method enables aggressive yet robust visual token reduction tailored to specific input classes.
papers.figure_caption: Impact of hyperparameter settings on MMVet dataset performance (LLaVA-v1.5-7B, 192 retained tokens). The heatmaps illustrate the score distribution across different question categories under varying conditions. Top: Evaluation of five representative layer-fusion strategies (A–E), ordered by an increasing proportion of weights assigned to deeper layers ( i.e. , shifting from shallow in A to deep in E). Bottom: Sweeping the attention–similarity mixing coefficient (higher: more attention; lower: more similarity).

papers.title: Combating Pattern and Content Bias: Adversarial Feature Learning for Generalized AI-Generated Image Detection
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Multi-dimensional Adversarial Feature Learning (MAFL) framework to improve the generalization of AI-generated image detection by combating data bias. MAFL uses a pretrained encoder and an adversarial bias-learning branch with a multi-dimensional adversarial loss. This method forces the model to learn features common across different generative models, rather than overfitting to specific patterns in the training data.
papers.figure_caption: (Color best viewed.) This illustration shows the problems of traditional methods under the asymmetric bias learning phenomenon and our proposed solution. (a) Traditional detectors (e.g., CLIP + classifier) tend to over-rely on the specific generative patterns of particular generative models when performing real/fake classification. (b) Such reliance on specific generative patterns makes the model prone to generalization failure when encountering unseen generative patterns. (c)–(e) MAFL introduces an adversarial mechanism between the real/fake classification task and the specific-pattern classification task, guiding the model to extract common generative features that characterize different generative models. These features are free from model-specific characteristics, thereby enabling stronger generalization when facing unseen generative models.

papers.title: Decoding by Perturbation: Mitigating MLLM Hallucinations via Dynamic Textual Perturbation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Decoding by Perturbation (DeP), a training-free framework to mitigate Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) hallucinations caused by language priors dominating visual evidence. DeP dynamically perturbs the input text during decoding to probe and suppress the influence of these latent priors by analyzing attention variance in the feature space. This method effectively enhances stable visual grounding without altering the original image distribution or compromising generative fluency.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of multimodal hallucination driven by text hypersensitivity. Although the modified prompts preserve the core semantics of the original query, minor variations in their surface structures lead to drastically different hallucinated outputs (e.g., “fake” or “red”). As revealed by the attention maps on the right, these textual perturbations cause a severe drift in visual grounding. Notably, the generation of “red” indicates that the model has established a spurious correlation, erroneously shifting its visual attention (bottom map) to the red hoodie rather than grounding it on the glasses.

papers.title: Don't Show Pixels, Show Cues: Unlocking Visual Tool Reasoning in Language Models via Perception Programs
papers.abstract: This paper argues that feeding raw, pixel-level outputs from vision tools hinders multimodal language models (MLLMs) because these representations conflict with the models' language-native strengths. The core contribution is **Perception Programs (P$^2$)**, a training-free method that transforms dense tool outputs into compact, structured, language-native summaries. This allows MLLMs to directly parse and effectively utilize the visual cues, leading to significant performance gains on perception-centric reasoning tasks.

papers.title: From Attenuation to Attention: Variational Information Flow Manipulation for Fine-Grained Visual Perception
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Variational Information Flow (VIF) framework to combat "Visual Attenuation," where fine-grained visual details are lost in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). VIF uses a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) to model question-relevant visual saliency as a latent distribution. This plug-and-play module actively manipulates information flow to preserve crucial fine-grained signals, improving performance on detailed perception tasks.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of Visual Attenuation. (Left) Schematic view showing visual tokens (blue) fading as they propagate through deep layers, while textual tokens (green) dominate. (Right) Quantitative layer-to-layer changes in vision attention ratio, averaged over 500 randomly sampled instances. The sharp drop in early layers indicates a premature loss of visual details.

papers.title: Generative Refinement Networks for Visual Synthesis
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Generative Refinement Networks (GRN) to overcome the inefficiency of diffusion models and the limitations of discrete tokenization in autoregressive (AR) models. GRN utilizes Hierarchical Binary Quantization (HBQ) for near-lossless representation, enabling an AR generation framework that progressively refines the output, similar to an artist's process. This approach achieves high-quality visual synthesis while incorporating complexity-aware sampling.
papers.figure_caption: Qualitative results for the class-to-image generation task.

papers.title: Habitat-GS: A High-Fidelity Navigation Simulator with Dynamic Gaussian Splatting
papers.abstract: Habitat-GS extends Habitat-Sim by integrating 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for high-fidelity, real-time photorealistic rendering. Its core contribution is enabling the simulation of dynamic, photorealistic human avatars using Gaussian Splats, allowing embodied AI agents to train effectively in human-populated environments while treating these avatars as realistic navigation obstacles.
papers.figure_caption: Habitat-GS is a navigation-centric embodied simulation platform with 3DGS and dynamic gaussian avatars. Compared to traditional mesh-based simulators (left), our 3DGS-based simulator (right) preserves high-frequency visual details and view-dependent effects, while gaussian avatars provide realistic and dynamic human presence for human-aware navigation scenarios, thus helping train more robust agents.

papers.title: Image-to-Image Translation Framework Embedded with Rotation Symmetry Priors
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a novel image-to-image translation (I2I) framework that incorporates rotation symmetry priors by utilizing rotation group equivariant convolutions. This method ensures the preservation of rotation symmetry, an intrinsic domain-invariant property, throughout the translation process. The core contribution is the development of a rotation equivariant I2I framework to enhance performance, particularly in unsupervised settings.
papers.figure_caption: (a) An input image for I2I containing numerous rotationally symmetric structures (illustrated using the iphone2dslr example). (b–c) Intermediate feature maps and translation results from a standard CNN, showing noticeable symmetry loss and structural artifacts. (d–e) Intermediate feature maps and translation results from the rotation equivariant network, demonstrating superior preservation of feature symmetry and producing higher-fidelity outputs.

papers.title: NTIRE 2026 The 3rd Restore Any Image Model (RAIM) Challenge: Professional Image Quality Assessment (Track 1)
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the NTIRE 2026 Track 1 challenge, focusing on **Professional Image Quality Assessment (IQA)**. The core method involves leveraging **Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs)** to move beyond scalar IQA scores and mimic human expert cognition when comparing high-quality images. The contribution is establishing a novel benchmark to test MLLMs' ability to provide nuanced, comparative reasoning for professional image evaluation.
papers.figure_caption: An example of pairwise comparison under the MCQ pattern, with annotations on the rationales behind the selection of the portrait part of the curated dataset.

papers.title: Nucleus-Image: Sparse MoE for Image Generation
papers.abstract: Nucleus-Image is a text-to-image generation model that achieves state-of-the-art quality while maintaining high efficiency by employing a sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) diffusion transformer architecture. Its core method involves using Expert-Choice Routing across 64 experts (totaling 17B parameters) but activating only about 2B per inference step. The contribution is establishing a new Pareto frontier in quality-versus-efficiency for image generation through this optimized sparse MoE design and architectural streamlining.
papers.figure_caption: Nucleus-Image generations of human subjects and portraits, spanning diverse cultures, ages, and artistic styles. From expressive character studies to fine-grained close-ups with intricate skin texture and detail.

papers.title: OFA-Diffusion Compression: Compressing Diffusion Model in One-Shot Manner
papers.abstract: This paper introduces OFA-Diffusion Compression, a one-shot training framework to compress Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DPMs) for deployment across various resource constraints without repeated training. The core method restricts the candidate subnetwork search space to a predefined set of parameter sizes, enabling the efficient generation of multiple compressed models simultaneously. This significantly reduces the overhead associated with training separate compressed models for different deployment scenarios.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Overview of our OFA-Diffusion Compression framework. On the left, we present the structure of U-ViT architecture. On the right, we show the details of layers in blocks (FFN and Self-Attention) in our compression framework. We omit GroupNorm and time embedding for simplicity. Given a retention rate 𝒫 i \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \) , only the top- 𝒫 i \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \) important parameters will be preserved in each block, then these budgets will be unevenly allocated to each layer in a greedy manner. By doing so, for two retention rates 𝒫 i < 𝒫 j \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \)<\( \mathcal{P}_{j} \) , in each layer, the corresponded module of 𝒫 i \( \mathcal{P}_{i} \) is a subset of the module corresponded to 𝒫 j \( \mathcal{P}_{j} \) .

papers.title: Probabilistic Feature Imputation and Uncertainty-Aware Multimodal Federated Aggregation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Probabilistic Feature Imputation Network (P-FIN) to address missing modalities in multimodal federated learning by generating imputed features along with calibrated uncertainty estimates. This uncertainty is used locally via sigmoid gating to weigh feature reliability before classification, and globally through Fed-UQ-Avg, an aggregation strategy that incorporates these reliability measures during model updates. The core contribution is providing uncertainty-aware imputation, enhancing the trustworthiness of collaborative models in safety-critical medical settings.

papers.title: Reading Between the Pixels: Linking Text-Image Embedding Alignment to Typographic Attack Success on Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper investigates typographic prompt injection attacks against Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by rendering adversarial text as images. The core contribution is demonstrating that attack success is highly sensitive to visual rendering parameters, particularly font size, showing a non-monotonic relationship where mid-range font sizes achieve peak effectiveness. This highlights the critical link between text-image embedding alignment and vulnerability to these visual adversarial attacks.
papers.figure_caption: Example typographic prompt injection attack using the TAP jailbreak method. (a) Harmful text prompt requesting attack planning guidance. (b) Prompt rendered as a 20px typographic image. (c) 30° rotation transformation increases L2 distance from text embedding.

papers.title: Redefining Quality Criteria and Distance-Aware Score Modeling for Image Editing Assessment
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **DS-IEQA**, a unified framework for Image Editing Quality Assessment that overcomes limitations in heuristic prompting and distance-agnostic scoring of existing MLLM methods. It achieves this through **Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO)** to automatically refine evaluation criteria and **Token-Decoupled Distance Regression Loss (TDRL)** to explicitly model the continuous structure of quality scores. The core contribution is a method that jointly learns evaluation criteria and score representations for more accurate and human-aligned IEQA.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO). The framework iteratively refines metric definitions via feedback signals to better align with implicit human evaluation criteria.

papers.title: ReflectCAP: Detailed Image Captioning with Reflective Memory
papers.abstract: ReflectCAP introduces a multi-agent pipeline to analyze a target Large Vision-Language Model's (LVLM) systematic errors (hallucinations and oversights) and distill these findings into **Structured Reflection Notes**. These notes then guide the captioning model at inference time, steering it to **avoid known inaccuracies while focusing on necessary details**, thereby improving both factuality and fine-grained coverage simultaneously. This method achieves a Pareto-optimal balance between these two metrics across various LVLMs and shows significant gains on automated caption evaluation benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of ReflectCAP. In the offline phase, a multi-agent reflective learning pipeline distills a target LVLM’s recurring captioning errors and omissions into Structured Reflection Notes. In the online phase, these notes guide caption generation for new images, producing captions that better balance factuality and coverage.

papers.title: Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention (PASA) to reduce the computational cost of Video Diffusion Transformers while eliminating flickering artifacts. PASA uses a curvature-aware dynamic budgeting mechanism to elastically allocate computation only during critical semantic transitions. This, combined with hardware-aligned grouped approximations, achieves efficient and temporally smooth high-fidelity video generation without retraining.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Visual and quantitative comparison on Wan 2.1-T2V-1.3B (quality vs. efficiency). PASA (ours) matches the end-to-end speedup of PISA ( Piecewise Sparse Attention ) while improving PSNR over other sparse attention baselines. Left: PASA preserves continuous strap geometry; other sparse methods show discontinuities. Right: PASA avoids the anatomical distortion present in competing sparse methods.

papers.title: SceneCritic: A Symbolic Evaluator for 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis
papers.abstract: SceneCritic is a novel symbolic evaluator designed to assess the spatial plausibility of 3D indoor scenes generated via LLMs/VLMs, moving beyond subjective rendered-view judgments. It achieves this by grounding its verification logic in SceneOnto, a structured spatial ontology built from existing 3D datasets. SceneCritic provides objective, object-level and relationship-level assessments of semantic, orientation, and geometric coherence, offering specific feedback on scene violations.
papers.figure_caption: VLM Evaluation vs. SceneCritic . Given a piano room with nine objects, the VLM assigns different scores to the same scene depending on which view is rendered, while SceneCritic evaluates the layout directly by traversing relational constraints from SceneOnto (e.g., incorrect table scales, chairs not facing the table they co-occur with). This produces stable, object-level assessments that identify specific violations alongside successful placements. SceneCritic scores also align more closely with human preferences.

papers.title: T2I-BiasBench: A Multi-Metric Framework for Auditing Demographic and Cultural Bias in Text-to-Image Models
papers.abstract: T2I-BiasBench is a novel, unified evaluation framework designed to audit Text-to-Image models for demographic bias, element omission, and cultural collapse simultaneously, using thirteen complementary metrics. The paper introduces seven new or adapted metrics to capture these three dimensions comprehensively across five prompt categories. This work provides the first framework to jointly assess these critical biases in diffusion models, benchmarking three open-source models against a reference baseline.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Bias and Fairness Evaluation Framework for text-to-image models. The pipeline consists of prompt generation (demographic and contextual), controlled image synthesis across multiple models, attribute extraction via vision-language methods, and a multi-metric evaluation module comprising 13 fairness, diversity, and alignment metrics. The framework produces composite bias and diversity scores, enabling comparative analysis of model behavior across demographic attributes and contextual scenarios.

papers.title: Task Alignment: A simple and effective proxy for model merging in computer vision
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Task Alignment** as a simple and effective proxy to accelerate hyperparameter selection for model merging in computer vision, especially when models have trainable, heterogeneous decoders. By using this proxy instead of costly downstream evaluation, the method significantly speeds up the process of merging models fine-tuned on different tasks while maintaining performance.
papers.figure_caption: (Left) Model merging methods produce a large number of candidates which depend on hyperparameters ( λ , μ ) (\( \lambda \),\( \mu \)) . (Middle) SOTA methods select hyperparameters based on validation performance. This is efficient for frozen decoders like CLIP (standard benchmarks) but becomes infeasible for more complex sets of tasks that require fine-tuning multiple task-specific decoders, for each hyperparameter candidate . (Right) Our proposed Task Alignment Proxy (TAP) can be used to select hyperparameters at a fraction of the cost while maintaining performance (see also Fig. ˜ 2 ).

papers.title: Towards Realistic and Consistent Orbital Video Generation via 3D Foundation Priors
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a method for generating geometrically realistic and consistent orbital videos from a single image by incorporating 3D shape priors. The core method leverages a 3D foundational generative model to provide structural guidance through global latent vectors and projected latent images, overcoming the limitations of pixel-wise attention for long-range view extrapolation. This approach ensures the generated videos maintain a plausible and coherent object structure throughout the orbit.

papers.title: A Sanity Check on Composed Image Retrieval
papers.abstract: This paper addresses the limitations of existing Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) benchmarks, which suffer from query ambiguity. The core method introduces **FISD**, a new, precisely controlled benchmark generated using diffusion models to eliminate ambiguity and allow for accurate evaluation across six dimensions. Furthermore, the authors propose an **automatic multi-round agentic evaluation framework** to assess CIR models in a sequential, interactive setting.

papers.title: ARGOS: Who, Where, and When in Agentic Multi-Camera Person Search
papers.abstract: ARGOS introduces a novel benchmark and framework that reframes multi-camera person search as an interactive reasoning task where an agent must strategically ask questions and use spatial/temporal tools to locate a target. The core method relies on a Spatio-Temporal Topology Graph (STTG) to ground reasoning about camera transitions under information asymmetry and a limited turn budget. This work significantly contributes by providing a challenging, multi-track benchmark that tests complex planning and reasoning abilities beyond standard perception in person search.
papers.figure_caption: Overview. Left: An ARGOS agent interacts with an ambiguous witness through multi-turn dialogue, combining appearance, spatial, and temporal queries. Right: The four-module agent architecture (Analyst → \( \to \) Planner → \( \to \) Interviewer → \( \to \) Interpreter) forms an observe-think-act loop over the evaluation environment.

papers.title: Relaxing Anchor-Frame Dominance for Mitigating Hallucinations in Video Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper addresses hallucinations in Video-LLMs by identifying and mitigating the tendency for the model to over-rely on a single "anchor frame" during generation, leading to temporally imbalanced evidence aggregation. The core method involves relaxing the dominance of this anchor frame, which is found to be a persistent, model-specific structural bias, thereby promoting a more balanced temporal evidence utilization to reduce hallucinations.

papers.title: StructDiff: A Structure-Preserving and Spatially Controllable Diffusion Model for Single-Image Generation
papers.abstract: StructDiff is a single-scale diffusion model for single-image generation that addresses limitations in structure preservation and spatial control. It introduces an **adaptive receptive field** module to maintain global and local image distributions, and incorporates **3D positional encoding** as a spatial prior for flexible control over the position and scale of generated content. This combination enables the synthesis of diverse images while accurately preserving the source image's structure.
papers.figure_caption: StructDiff enables diverse generation and controllable editing from a single training image. Top : Diverse generation at arbitrary scales. Middle : Controllable generation through PE-guided geometric editing and mask-guided fine-tuning. Bottom : Various applications without retraining. Results demonstrate effectiveness in structural preservation and spatial controllability.

papers.title: Towards Long-horizon Agentic Multimodal Search
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **LMM-Searcher**, a novel framework for long-horizon multimodal search agents designed to overcome context explosion and high token costs. The core method involves **offloading visual assets to an external file system and representing them with lightweight textual identifiers (UIDs)**, allowing for on-demand visual loading via a specialized fetch tool. This approach effectively manages heterogeneous information over extended tasks while preserving crucial visual signals.
papers.figure_caption: An illustration of LMM-Searcher. For simplicity, we employ simple strings as uids in this figure. In real implementation, we use URLs. This figure serves solely as a functional demonstration and does not represent any actual search results.

papers.title: ViLL-E: Video LLM Embeddings for Retrieval
papers.abstract: ViLL-E introduces a novel embedding generation mechanism within a unified VideoLLM architecture designed to enhance performance on video retrieval tasks where standard VideoLLMs struggle. The core method involves a three-stage training process combining generative and contrastive learning, enabling the model to dynamically adjust its processing time based on video complexity. This approach significantly improves performance across various video understanding tasks, particularly temporal localization and retrieval.

papers.title: Visual Preference Optimization with Rubric Rewards
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **rDPO (rubric-based Direct Preference Optimization)**, a novel framework that leverages instance-specific, checklist-style rubrics to generate high-quality preference data for multimodal tasks. By scoring responses against these detailed criteria, rDPO captures fine-grained quality differences better than traditional outcome-based signals. This method significantly improves the performance of reward models and enhances downstream task performance compared to existing preference optimization pipelines.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of rDPO. The top lane constructs a model-agnostic instruction-rubric pool by mining challenging seeds via model ensembles and drafting instance-specific rubrics with structured “essential” and “additional” criteria. The bottom lane achieves on-policy data curation and optimization by sampling responses from a target policy, scoring them via rubric-grounded judging, and mining preference pairs to drive iterative DPO.

papers.title: Every Picture Tells a Dangerous Story: Memory-Augmented Multi-Agent Jailbreak Attacks on VLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **MemJack**, a novel memory-augmented multi-agent framework for jailbreaking Vision-Language Models (VLMs). MemJack's core method explicitly leverages the **semantic structures within natural images** by using coordinated agents to map visual entities to malicious intents and generate adversarial prompts. This contributes by exposing a previously unscrutinized, deep semantic attack surface beyond simple pixel or typographic manipulations.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Comparison of VLM jailbreak paradigms. Existing attacks use (a) textual manipulation, (b) visual perturbations, (c) typographic or (d) harmful images. (e) Ours MemJack exploits original natural images via multi-agent visual-semantic camouflage with memory-augmented reflection.

papers.title: QuarkMedSearch: A Long-Horizon Deep Search Agent for Exploring Medical Intelligence
papers.abstract: QuarkMedSearch is a specialized deep search agent built upon Tongyi DeepResearch, tailored for complex, multi-hop medical inquiries in Chinese. Its core method involves constructing long-horizon medical deep search training data by integrating a medical knowledge graph with online exploration. The contribution lies in presenting a full-pipeline approach—including data construction, a two-stage SFT/RL training strategy, and new benchmarks—to significantly advance agent performance in this vertical domain.
papers.figure_caption: Medical Deep Search Task Synthesis Pipeline.

papers.title: SOAR: Self-Correction for Optimal Alignment and Refinement in Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: SOAR addresses the exposure bias in diffusion model post-training by introducing a self-correction mechanism that bridges the gap between supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). The core method involves performing a single, stop-gradient rollout from a real sample, re-noising the resulting intermediate state, and then using this newly generated noisy state as a target for the model to predict the original noise, effectively training the model to correct its own trajectory errors. This contributes a novel, efficient bias-correction technique that refines alignment without relying on sparse terminal rewards.
papers.figure_caption: Conceptual comparison of post-training regimes from a trajectory perspective. Left: SFT trains only on ground-truth denoising states. Once an inference rollout exits the narrow corridor of well-optimized states, subsequent steps sharpen the wrong structure. Middle: RL separates good and bad final outcomes with a terminal reward, but the sparse signal arrives after the erroneous rollout has already formed. Right: SOAR performs a one-step rollout to generate off-trajectory states and supervises the model to correct back toward the clean target, acting before semantic errors fully materialize.

papers.title: Coding-Free and Privacy-Preserving MCP Framework for Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS), which automates the complex clinical research workflow using Large Language Models (LLMs) orchestrated via a Model Context Protocol (MCP). The core contribution is a **coding-free, privacy-preserving framework** where sensitive data remains secured within the MCP server, allowing users to drive the entire research pipeline (planning, cohort construction, analysis) solely through natural language commands.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS). Given a user intent, CARIS autonomously executes a multi-step clinical research workflow by interacting with agents, heterogeneous datasets, and various tools, while allowing user intervention. Users do not have direct access to the file or DB systems.

papers.title: Development, Evaluation, and Deployment of a Multi-Agent System for Thoracic Tumor Board
papers.abstract: This paper describes the development and deployment of an automated, multi-agent AI system to generate concise patient summaries for the Stanford Thoracic Tumor Board, replacing a manual process. The authors evaluated various automated summarization methods against physician gold standards and fact-based metrics, ultimately deploying the best-performing tool into routine clinical practice. A key contribution is demonstrating the successful integration of AI workflows into clinical settings and validating the use of LLMs as effective evaluation judges.

papers.title: Modality-Native Routing in Agent-to-Agent Networks: A Multimodal A2A Protocol Extension
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **MMA2A**, a protocol extension for Agent-to-Agent (A2A) networks that implements **modality-native routing**. This method routes multimodal signals (voice, image, text) directly in their original format based on agent capability declarations. The core contribution is demonstrating that this native routing significantly improves task accuracy (by 20 percentage points over text-bottleneck methods), but only when the receiving agent possesses the necessary multimodal reasoning capabilities.

papers.title: SpecBound: Adaptive Bounded Self-Speculation with Layer-wise Confidence Calibration
papers.abstract: SpecBound introduces an adaptive self-speculation framework for LLMs that addresses overconfident early predictions via layer-wise temperature annealing for early exit decisions. It adaptively bounds speculation length based on token difficulty and efficiently reprocesses draft hidden states in a unified parallel pass. This method significantly accelerates inference while maintaining exact output equivalence without modifying the base LLM.
papers.figure_caption: Layer-wise token prediction and confidence of a segment with the MT-Bench prompt. Each cell shows the greedy-decoded token and its confidence (color intensity: darker = higher probability) at a given layer. Gray cells indicate that the token has already correctly exited at an earlier layer and is no longer considered for early-exit decisions. This figure reveals the issues of spurious high-confidence predictions and heterogeneous exit depths.

papers.title: VERITAS: Verifiable Epistemic Reasoning for Image-Derived Hypothesis Testing via Agentic Systems
papers.abstract: VERITAS is a multi-agent system designed to autonomously test natural-language hypotheses on multimodal clinical data, streamlining the fragmented process of clinical discovery. Its core contribution is providing a fully auditable evidence trail for every statistical conclusion, linking the final verdict back to inspectable outputs like segmentation masks and code. Furthermore, it employs an epistemic evidence label framework to classify outcomes as Supported, Refuted, Underpowered, or Invalid based on significance, effect direction, and power.
papers.figure_caption: Veritas architecture and evidence flow. Given a natural language hypothesis and dataset, three role-specialized agents (PI, Imaging Specialist, Statistician) with a Critic collaborate through four phases: (1) collaborative analysis planning producing a structured plan; (2A) neural segmentation, grounding all evidence in image-derived masks; (2B) sandboxed code generation and execution yielding statistical outputs; and (3) interpretation of the Phase 2B statistical outputs in the context of the accumulated workflow history to produce a verdict. The Evidence Classification Operator (ECO) mechanically derives the evidence label from Phase 2B outputs. Every phase produces a versioned artifact (right), forming a complete provenance trail from hypothesis to conclusion.
